Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

After spending try out a few things I haven't found a nice way to apply this on a per-inode basis due to the current layering.  I'm still convinced something like this is needed when the are a large number of core in the systems, but for the moment I'm abandoning this change.

Patch Set 2:

Brian, does this need to be a per-inode limit, or is it enough that you selectively avoid acquiring the spinlock if it is already held?  Then whenever one thread is already in the cleanup context it can do the needed work (and maybe a bit more than needed), and the other threads can just go back to work on something else?

parents.subject: LU-18053 osc: add another cond_resched() to osc_lru_shrink()

commit.subject: LU-18053 osc: add active osc_lru_shrink() limit
commit.message:
LU-18053 osc: add active osc_lru_shrink() limit

When discarding pages osc_lru_shrink() eventually calls
delete_from_page_cache() which must aquire a spin lock
on the page cache.  On systems with a large number of
CPUs this can result spin lock contention and soft
lockups.

In order to prevent this apply a per-filesystem limit
to the number of concurrent osc_lru_shrink() callers.
This relys on osc_lru_shrink() being called frequently
enough per OSC that under normal conditions there will
be a less contended opperunity to run.  When the force
flag is passed to osc_lru_shrink() it will block until
until under the maximum limit.  No attempt is made at
fairness.

A default limit of 4 was used to allow some concurrency
without risking overwhelming the page cache spin lock.

If the lock contention could be further reduced, for
example by implementing LU-12534 then it's likely the per
filesystem osc_lru_shrink() limit could be increased.

NOTES:
- This patch uses the kernel attributes interface instead
  of debugfs.  Its not clear to me which interface is
  preferred, I'm happy to update it either way.

Signed-off-by: Brian Behlendorf <behlendorf1@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: Ic6be5d8b42d9fb44c2b532f268c03486fc0df8b5
